executiveSummary:
# EXECUTIVE SUMMARY: Medicaid Program Performance Analysis

**Enrollment and Financial Trends**

National Medicaid enrollment reached 74.9 million beneficiaries as of February 2026, with all major states experiencing year-over-year declines as the post-pandemic unwinding continues. Texas, with 4.0 million enrollees, ranks third nationally but shows a moderate 3.8% decrease compared to more dramatic reductions in California (6.8%) and Michigan (7.6%). National program expenditures totaled $215.9 billion in fiscal year 2024, averaging $2,883 per enrollee. Texas demonstrates relatively efficient cost management at $3,014 per enrollee, positioning favorably against high-cost states like Pennsylvania ($4,363) and Massachusetts ($4,221), while exceeding the efficiency seen in Florida ($2,036) and North Carolina ($2,623). The state's robust managed care penetration of 93.3% significantly exceeds the national average of 83.2%, covering 3.9 million beneficiaries and likely contributing to cost containment efforts.

**Quality Performance and Regulatory Environment**

Texas quality performance remains in the moderate tier, scoring 62 out of 100 and ranking 30th nationally, falling below the national average of 65.6. This places the state well behind top performers like Massachusetts (81) and Minnesota (80) but ahead of concerning underperformers in the Southeast, including Mississippi (48) and Alabama (52). The current regulatory landscape indicates heightened federal oversight, with recent CMS activities including community engagement requirements, managed care payment reforms, and provider enrollment moratoria that may impact state program operations. Texas leadership should prioritize quality improvement initiatives to advance from moderate performance while leveraging the state's strong managed care infrastructure and cost efficiency as foundations for enhanced care delivery and outcomes measurement.
texasSpotlight:
## Texas Medicaid Program Performance Analysis

Texas operates the nation's third-largest Medicaid program with nearly 4 million enrollees, representing 5.3% of national enrollment despite having 9% of the U.S. population—a reflection of the state's decision not to expand Medicaid under the ACA. The program shows moderate performance across key metrics, with per-enrollee spending of $3,014 sitting just above the national average of $2,883 but well below expansion states like Pennsylvania ($4,363) and Massachusetts ($4,221). Texas's enrollment declined 3.8% year-over-year, consistent with the national trend of unwinding pandemic-era continuous eligibility protections, and tracking closely with peer non-expansion states like Florida (-4.2%) and Georgia (-3.9%). At 93.3% managed care penetration, Texas significantly exceeds the national average of 83.2%, demonstrating the state's strong commitment to managed care delivery models.

However, Texas faces notable quality challenges that warrant close attention. The state's quality score of 62/100 ranks 30th nationally and falls in the "moderate" tier, trailing southern peer states like North Carolina and even non-expansion state Florida. This quality gap, combined with the state's large uninsured population due to non-expansion status, represents a critical area for improvement. Federal regulatory activity around community engagement requirements and state-directed payments could significantly impact Texas's program operations, particularly given the state's heavy reliance on managed care. The convergence of ongoing federal oversight, quality performance pressures, and the political dynamics around potential Medicaid expansion will likely define Texas's Medicaid trajectory over the next several years, making it essential to monitor whether the state can improve health outcomes while maintaining its cost-effective managed care approach.

alerts.title: Pennsylvania Per-Enrollee Spending 51% Above National Average
alerts.description: Pennsylvania's per-enrollee spending of $4,363 is significantly higher than the national average of $2,883, indicating potential cost control issues.

alerts.title: Florida Extremely Low Per-Enrollee Spending Raises Quality Concerns
alerts.description: Florida's per-enrollee spending of $2,036 is 29% below national average despite having 3.6M enrollees, suggesting potential underfunding of care.

alerts.title: Five States Show Critically Low Quality Performance
alerts.description: Arkansas, South Carolina, Alabama, Oklahoma, and Mississippi all scored below 55/100 on quality measures, well below the national average of 65.6.

alerts.title: Massachusetts Achieves Top Quality Score with High Spending
alerts.description: Massachusetts leads in quality performance (81/100) while maintaining high per-enrollee spending ($4,221), demonstrating effective resource utilization.

alerts.title: Multiple High-Enrollment States Show Concerning Quality Gaps
alerts.description: Large states including Texas (62/100) and Ohio show quality scores below the national average despite significant enrollment and spending levels.

executiveInsights.title: Texas Medicaid enrollment declined 3.8% YoY despite highest managed care penetration
executiveInsights.summary: Texas lost ~156K enrollees with 3.8% YoY decline while maintaining 93.3% managed care penetration (10 points above national average). This creates revenue pressure on MCO partners and potential system capacity issues.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Enrollment decline directly impacts contract volumes and MCO financial performance, requiring immediate assessment of operational scaling and partner support strategies.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Schedule urgent call with HHSC to assess enrollment projections and MCO financial impacts

executiveInsights.title: Texas quality score at 62/100 ranks #30 nationally, below CMS expectations
executiveInsights.summary: Texas scores 62/100 on quality measures, ranking 30th of 50 states in moderate tier. This is 13.6 points below top performers and signals potential CMS scrutiny.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Poor quality performance triggers federal oversight, funding adjustments, and puts managed care contracts at risk of penalties or non-renewal.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag quality improvement as priority workstream for Q2 planning

executiveInsights.title: CMS announcing temporary enrollment moratoria affecting provider networks
executiveInsights.summary: Multiple Federal Register entries show CMS implementing nationwide temporary moratoria on provider enrollment in Medicare and Medicaid programs. This directly impacts network adequacy requirements.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Provider enrollment freezes will constrain Texas network expansion and may trigger contract compliance issues around network adequacy standards.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review provider network adequacy compliance implications with HHSC legal team

executiveInsights.title: Texas per-enrollee costs at $3,014 exceed national average by $131
executiveInsights.summary: Texas spends $3,014 per enrollee compared to $2,883 national average, representing 4.5% premium. Total expenditure of $12B makes Texas the 4th highest spending state.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Higher per-capita costs indicate either service intensity issues or cost management opportunities that could be addressed through operational improvements.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Analyze cost drivers vs peer states for potential efficiency opportunities

executiveInsights.title: New CMS state directed payments rule may impact Texas MCO reimbursement
executiveInsights.summary: Federal Register shows new rule on "Medicaid Managed Care State Directed Payments" that could affect how Texas structures MCO reimbursements. Rule published May 22, 2026.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: State directed payment changes could require contract modifications and impact MCO financial models that Accenture helps manage.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review new state directed payments rule for Texas contract implications

riskOpportunity.label: Enrollment decline acceleration
riskOpportunity.description: Texas Medicaid enrollment dropping 3.8% YoY with potential for steeper declines affecting contract value and scope.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop enrollment stabilization analytics and targeted outreach solutions for HHSC.

riskOpportunity.label: Quality improvement mandate
riskOpportunity.description: Texas ranks #30 in quality measures creating pressure for improvement initiatives and potential penalties.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Propose comprehensive quality analytics platform to move Texas into top performer tier.

riskOpportunity.label: Federal regulatory shifts
riskOpportunity.description: Multiple CMS rule changes around community engagement and state directed payments creating compliance uncertainty.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Establish dedicated regulatory compliance monitoring and advisory service for HHSC.

riskOpportunity.label: Managed care optimization
riskOpportunity.description: Texas has 93.3% MC penetration above national average, creating opportunities for advanced managed care tools.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop next-generation managed care oversight and optimization platform leveraging high penetration rates.

riskOpportunity.label: Procurement cycle timing
riskOpportunity.description: Active HHSC procurement opportunities with potential contract renewals creating competitive positioning challenges.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Accelerate strategic positioning and capability demonstrations ahead of major procurement cycles.

riskOpportunity.label: Per-enrollee cost pressure
riskOpportunity.description: Texas per-enrollee spending at $3,014 near national average with declining enrollment creating budget strain.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Design cost optimization analytics to help HHSC maintain efficiency with shrinking enrollment base.

riskOpportunity.label: OIG oversight intensification
riskOpportunity.description: Active HHS OIG Medicaid portfolio oversight creating compliance risks but also demand for audit readiness solutions.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Build proactive compliance monitoring capabilities to help HHSC stay ahead of OIG scrutiny.

riskOpportunity.label: Digital transformation gap
riskOpportunity.description: Provider enrollment moratoriums and data collection changes demanding enhanced digital capabilities from state systems.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Propose comprehensive digital modernization roadmap addressing enrollment, provider management, and data collection.

pulseMetrics.label: Total Enrollment
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas Medicaid enrollment decreased by 3.8% year-over-year to 3,997,752 enrollees as of February 2026.

pulseMetrics.label: Enrollment Change
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas experienced a 3.8% year-over-year decline in Medicaid enrollment, slightly better than the national average.

pulseMetrics.label: Per Enrollee Spend
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas spends $3,014 per Medicaid enrollee annually, above the national average of $2,883.

pulseMetrics.label: Managed Care Rate
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas has 93.3% managed care penetration, significantly above the national average of 83.2%.

pulseMetrics.label: Quality Score
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas scored 62 out of 100 on quality measures, ranking 30th nationally in the moderate performance tier.

pulseMetrics.label: Active Procurements
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Two active procurement opportunities are currently available through Texas HHSC procurement portals.

pulseMetrics.label: OIG/Regulatory Items
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Eight regulatory and oversight items are currently active from HHS OIG and Federal Register sources.

pulseMetrics.label: Next Milestone
pulseMetrics.tooltip: No specific upcoming milestone dates are available in the current data.
